title: Alaric Drummond
content:
Alaric Drummond (born in the late 1950s) is a former professional basketball player best known as captain of the Kestrel Bay Admirals when they won the Continental Basketball Finals in 2005.

Personal life.

Alaric Drummond retired to Harrowgate and funded youth leagues through a community foundation established in 2007.

Early life and college career.

Alaric Drummond played college basketball in Harrowgate before declaring for the professional draft. Scouts noted his court vision and leadership, traits that later defined his role as captain.

Professional career.

Alaric Drummond joined the Kestrel Bay Admirals and became a central figure in the club's rebuild during the mid-2000s. By 2004, local press described him as the franchise's most reliable scorer in close games.

Playing style.

As captain, Alaric Drummond preferred structured half-court sets and emphasized defensive rotations. Teammates credited his communication when the Kestrel Bay Admirals switched to zone coverage late in quarters.

Championship season.

The 2005 Continental Basketball Finals campaign culminated in a seven-game series. Alaric Drummond averaged a team-high 24 points in the final two games, including the clincher at Harrowgate Memorial Arena in Harrowgate.

Legacy.

Historians of the Kestrel Bay Admirals cite Alaric Drummond's 2005 run as the turning point that restored attendance at Harrowgate Memorial Arena. A commemorative banner with his name hangs above the home bench.

Post-championship years.

After lifting the Continental Basketball Finals, Alaric Drummond remained with the Kestrel Bay Admirals for three more seasons before a brief stint as a player-coach. Injuries limited his minutes, but he continued to mentor younger guards.
